bird.name: 加拿大威尔逊森莺
bird.summary: 加拿大威尔逊森莺（学名：Cardellina canadensis）为雀形目森莺科下的一个鸟种, 分布于美洲 。其种加词“canadensis”意为“加拿大的”。 参考文献

locations.name: 美國東北部
locations.summary: 美國東北部為美国人口调查局所定義的美国地區。美國東北部北臨加拿大，西臨中西部，南接美國南部，東向大西洋。此區域乃美國工商業最發達的區域及都市化程度最高的區域，美國第一大都會紐約市即位於該區。 根據人口調查局的定義，美國的東北部包括9個州：…

locations.name: 育空地區
locations.summary: 育空（英语、法语：Yukon）是加拿大13個一級行政區中的三個地区（Territory）之一，位於加拿大的西北方。根據2016年的人口普查，育空人口有35,874人，其中25,085人，70%的人居住於育空首府白马市（Whitehorse…

locations.name: 圭亚那
locations.summary: 蓋亞那合作共和国（英語：Co-operative Republic of Guyana），通称圭亚那（英語：Guyana），位於南美洲北部，是南美洲一个以英語為官方語言的国家，也是英联邦成员國。東鄰蘇利南，南臨巴西，西鄰委内瑞拉，北鄰大西…

locations.name: 玻利維亞
locations.summary: 玻利維亞多民族國（西班牙語：Estado Plurinacional de Bolivia），通稱玻利維亞，是位於南美洲中西部的內陸國家，與巴西、秘魯、智利、阿根廷、巴拉圭五國接壤，亦為南美洲國家聯盟的成員國；其法定首都為蘇克雷，但實際上…

locations.name: 明尼蘇達州
locations.summary: 明尼蘇達州（State of Minnesota，/ˌmɪnəˈsoʊtə/ MIN-ə-SOH-tə）是美國第32個州，於1858年5月11日加入聯邦。該州位於美國中西部的上中西部地區，北接加拿大曼尼托巴省與安大略省，東鄰威斯康辛州，南…

relations.subject: 加拿大威尔逊森莺
relations.evidence: It summers in Canada and northeastern United States and winters in northern South America

relations.subject: 加拿大威尔逊森莺
relations.evidence: During the breeding season 82% of the population can be found in Canada and 18% in the United States. In Canada, the summer range extends from southeastern Yukon to Nova Scotia

relations.subject: 加拿大威尔逊森莺
relations.evidence: state)|Georgia]]. In winter, the Canada warbler's range extends from Guyana to northwestern Bolivia around the northern and western side of the Andean crest. In both summer and winter seasons, the Canada warbler inhabits moist thickets

relations.subject: 加拿大威尔逊森莺
relations.evidence: In the northwestern parts of its range, it frequents aspen forests; in the center of the range, it is found in forested wetlands and swamps; and in the south, it occupies montane rhododendron thickets." In the winter, it prefers mid- and upper-elevation habitats. In northern Minnesota, a study found that Canada warblers inhabited the shrub-forest edge, rather than mature forests or open fields with shrub. In New England, the Canada warbler was found to be "disturbance specialists" moving into patches of forests recovering from wind throw or timber removal. Because of its preference for low-height foraging in deciduous forests, it may be bounded at higher elevations as suitable habitat disappears and it suffers competition from the black-throated blue warbler which prefers similar habitats. Two accidentals have been observed in Europe

relations.object_summary: 具备成熟林冠和树洞资源的森林生境。
